title: Ecosystem Layers
editorialSummary: Animals grouped by their primary ecosystem layer — canopy, forest floor, deep ocean, or tidal zone; the false trail is that the mudskipper breathes air and moves on land using its fins, tempting solvers to place it in the forest-floor group with the giant anteater and tapir, but the mudskipper is a tidal-zone fish whose entire life cycle depends on mangrove mudflats.
